The Asheville and Blue Ridge region sits at the crossroads of the Southern Appalachians, where the Blue Ridge Parkway winds through some of the oldest mountains on Earth. The city of Asheville anchors the area with its eclectic downtown, farm-to-table dining, and a music scene that punches well above its weight.

Beyond the city limits, the landscape opens into a patchwork of national forests, state parks, and private mountain retreats. Elevations range from 2,000 to over 6,000 feet, creating microclimates that support everything from rhododendron tunnels to high-altitude balds with 40-mile views. Fall foliage here peaks later than the surrounding lowlands, extending the color season well into November.

A network of eleven glacially carved lakes in central New York, surrounded by rolling vineyards, dramatic gorge trails, and charming lakeside villages that define the heart of the state's wine country.
Watkins Glen State Park gorge trail
Ithaca's gorges and Cornell Botanic Gardens

The Finger Lakes region takes its name from eleven long, narrow lakes that stretch like fingers across central New York, carved by glaciers during the last ice age. The deepest of these, Seneca Lake, plunges over 600 feet and creates a microclimate that sustains one of the most productive wine regions in the eastern United States.

Over 200 wineries line the lake shores, specializing in cool-climate Rieslings, Gewürztraminers, and sparkling wines that rival their European counterparts. Between tastings, visitors find farm-to-table restaurants, craft breweries, and cideries tucked into the rolling hillsides.

The landscape is defined by dramatic gorges cut through layers of shale and limestone. Watkins Glen State Park packs nineteen waterfalls into a two-mile trail that winds behind curtains of falling water. Taughannock Falls drops 215 feet — taller than Niagara — into a glacial plunge pool surrounded by towering cliff walls. The gorge trails around Ithaca offer dozens more cascades accessible by short hikes from town.

Beyond the gorges, the region offers quiet lake scenery perfect for kayaking, paddleboarding, and sunset watching from rocky shorelines. Properties here range from lakefront cottages with private docks to hillside retreats overlooking vineyard-striped valleys, all within easy reach of the region's food and wine scene.

A dramatic landscape of sandstone gorges, recessed caves, and cascading waterfalls in southeastern Ohio, offering year-round hiking and a growing luxury cabin scene.
Old Man's Cave and Ash Cave gorge trails
John Glenn Astronomy Park (dark sky site)
Local wineries and Hocking Hills dining

Hocking Hills is southeastern Ohio's best-kept secret — a region where ancient sandstone has been sculpted by millions of years of water into deep gorges, recess caves, and towering rock formations draped in hemlock and fern. The landscape feels more like the Pacific Northwest than the Midwest, with moss-covered cliffs and waterfalls tucked into every hollow.

## The Landscape

The region centers on Hocking Hills State Park, where six major hiking areas showcase the geological drama. Old Man's Cave follows a gorge carved by Old Man Creek, passing through tunnels and under overhangs before reaching a waterfall-fed pool. Ash Cave is the largest recess cave in Ohio — a horseshoe-shaped alcove 700 feet wide with a 90-foot waterfall dropping from its lip. Conkle's Hollow is a box canyon with 200-foot sandstone walls rising on either side of a narrow trail, while Cedar Falls offers one of the most photographed cascades in the state.

## Attractions and Activities

Beyond the state park trails, Hocking Hills has developed into a year-round destination. The Hocking Hills Canopy Walk stretches over the forest canopy on a suspended bridge system, offering aerial views of the gorge landscape. John Glenn Astronomy Park, designated as a dark sky site, hosts stargazing events throughout the year. The region's growing food and wine scene includes local wineries, craft breweries in nearby Logan, and farm-to-table restaurants that have followed the tourism boom.

## The Retreat Appeal

A wave of luxury cabin development has transformed Hocking Hills into one of the Midwest's premier retreat destinations. Properties range from architect-designed modern cabins with floor-to-ceiling glass walls to rustic-luxe treehouses perched above the forest floor. Most feature hot tubs, fire pits, and decks oriented toward wooded ravines. The region's proximity to Columbus (under 90 minutes) and reasonable drive times from cities across the eastern Midwest make it accessible for long weekends without requiring a flight.

America's newest national park protects one of the oldest rivers on the continent, carving a 1,000-foot gorge through the Appalachian Plateau with world-class rock climbing and whitewater rafting.
New River Gorge Bridge (3,030 feet long)

New River Gorge became America's newest national park in 2020, though the New River itself is paradoxically one of the oldest rivers in the world. The river has carved a gorge nearly 1,000 feet deep through the Appalachian Plateau, creating dramatic sandstone cliffs that attract rock climbers from around the globe.

The park offers over 70,000 acres of protected forest, with trails ranging from easy riverside walks to strenuous rim-to-river descents. The New River Gorge Bridge, at 876 feet above the river, is the third-highest bridge in the United States and hosts Bridge Day each October when BASE jumpers leap from its span. Whitewater rafting on the New and Gauley Rivers draws paddlers seeking everything from family-friendly Class III rapids to expert-level Class V drops. The surrounding towns of Fayetteville and Beckley provide a laid-back mountain culture with local restaurants and gear shops.

America's most visited national park straddles the Tennessee-North Carolina border, with misty peaks, old-growth forests, and over 800 miles of trails through diverse Appalachian ecosystems.

The Great Smoky Mountains are named for the natural fog that hangs in the valleys, created by the dense vegetation releasing moisture into the cool mountain air. This UNESCO World Heritage Site protects over 500,000 acres of temperate rainforest, making it one of the most biodiverse places in North America.

The region surrounding the park offers a mix of rustic mountain culture and modern comfort. Gatlinburg and Pigeon Forge sit at the Tennessee entrance, while the quieter North Carolina side provides access through Cherokee and Bryson City. Properties here range from secluded ridgetop cabins to creekside retreats tucked into hollows that feel miles from civilization.

A dramatic plateau landscape on the Cumberland Plateau in Tennessee, featuring deep gorges, sandstone cliffs, and pristine waterfalls surrounded by the University of the South's 13,000-acre domain.

South Cumberland sits atop the Cumberland Plateau where ancient sandstone has been carved into dramatic gorges, natural bridges, and cascading waterfalls. The area centers around Sewanee, home to the University of the South, whose 13,000-acre Domain is laced with hiking trails open to the public.

The Fiery Gizzard Trail is consistently ranked among the best day hikes in the Southeast, threading along cliff edges and behind waterfalls for 12 miles. Foster Falls offers a 60-foot cascade with a swimming hole at its base, while Savage Gulf provides multi-day backpacking through some of Tennessee's wildest terrain. The plateau's elevation keeps summer temperatures 10-15 degrees cooler than the surrounding valleys.

The Shenandoah Valley stretches 200 miles between Virginia's two major mountain ranges, creating a corridor of farmland, orchards, and small towns backed by forested ridges. Shenandoah National Park runs along the crest of the Blue Ridge above, accessible via Skyline Drive and its 75 overlooks.

Old Rag Mountain draws hikers from across the mid-Atlantic for its granite scramble and 360-degree summit views. Below the park, the Shenandoah River winds through limestone country perfect for lazy kayak floats. The valley's lower elevation means milder winters and an extended growing season that supports a thriving wine industry. Properties in this region range from converted barns in the valley floor to mountaintop retreats with views stretching to the Alleghenies.

## Why Fall Is Peak Retreat Season

The southern Appalachians put on one of the longest foliage shows in the eastern US — color rolls from the highest ridges in late September down through the valleys into early November. These curated stays put you right in the canopy.

### What to Expect

- **Peak color window:** Mid-October through early November at elevations between 3,000 and 5,000 feet
- **Golden hour magic:** West-facing properties catch sunset light filtering through turning leaves
- **Cooler nights:** Perfect hot tub weather with temps dropping into the 40s after dark

### Planning Tips

Book at least 6 weeks ahead for peak weekends. Midweek stays offer the same views with more availability and quieter trails. The Blue Ridge Parkway closes sections for weather — check conditions before planning scenic drives.

## Featured Stays for Fall Color

Each of these properties sits at elevation with unobstructed tree canopy views, a private hot tub, and sedan-friendly access so you can focus on the color instead of the road.

### Glamping Collective Ultra Luxe Dome

A geodesic dome at 4,334 feet with panoramic Blue Ridge views through transparent panels. Watch the color change from your heated floors or the outdoor hot tub.

### Canopy Ridge Treehouse

Perched among the hardwoods in the Smokies, this treehouse puts you at eye level with the turning canopy. The wraparound deck is prime leaf-peeping territory.

### Villa Luna Mirror Cabin

The mirrored exterior reflects the surrounding forest — in fall, the cabin itself becomes part of the foliage display. North Georgia color peaks slightly later, extending your window.

## Waterfalls Within Reach

The southern Appalachians hold hundreds of waterfalls, many accessible via short hikes from trailheads less than an hour from these properties. Summer heat makes the cold mountain water feel perfect, and afternoon thunderstorms keep the falls running strong through August.

### What to Look For

- **Proximity to falls:** Each featured property is within 45 minutes of at least two major waterfall hikes
- **Swimming access:** Many falls in this region have legal swimming holes at their base
- **Morning timing:** Hit popular falls before 9 AM to avoid crowds and afternoon storms
- **Trail difficulty:** Most waterfall hikes here are moderate — under 3 miles round trip with well-maintained paths

### Summer Planning Tips

Weekday visits to popular falls like Triple Falls or Laurel Falls mean fewer crowds. Always check water levels after heavy rain — some swimming holes become dangerous in high water. Bring water shoes for slippery creek crossings.

## Featured Stays Near Waterfalls

### Glamping Collective Ultra Luxe Dome

DuPont State Forest and its Triple Falls are a 45-minute drive. The property's own trails connect to creek swimming spots accessible only to guests.

### Canopy Ridge Treehouse

The Smokies offer dozens of waterfall hikes within an hour. Laurel Falls, Grotto Falls, and Rainbow Falls are all accessible from this base, with the hot tub waiting after a day on the trail.

### Villa Luna Mirror Cabin

North Georgia's Amicalola Falls — the tallest cascading waterfall in the Southeast — is nearby. Several lesser-known swimming holes along the Toccoa River are within a 30-minute drive.

## The Case for Winter Retreats

Winter strips the mountains to their bones. Without leaf cover, ridgeline views stretch further, stargazing improves dramatically, and the contrast of hot water against cold air makes every soak feel earned. These properties are designed for exactly this.

### What Makes a Great Winter Stay

- **Hot tub as centerpiece:** Every featured property has a private hot tub accessible in any weather
- **Heated interiors:** Radiant floors, fireplaces, or efficient heating systems keep the cabin warm without drafts
- **Sedan-friendly access:** No chains or 4WD required — all roads are maintained through winter
- **Shorter drive times:** Off-peak means less traffic on mountain roads

### Winter Planning Notes

January and February offer the best rates and availability. Light snow is possible above 3,500 feet but rarely accumulates enough to close roads. Pack layers for the walk between cabin and hot tub — the temperature difference is part of the experience.

## The Experience

The Floating Mirror Cabin is Bolt Farm's most architecturally daring unit. Clad in one-way mirrored panels, the structure reflects its surroundings so completely that it appears to hover — a shimmering ghost among the trees. From inside, every surface is a window into the forest. The cabin is elevated off the ground, adding to the floating illusion and providing unobstructed sightlines in every direction.

## What Makes It Special

- **Disappearing architecture**: Mirror cladding renders the cabin nearly invisible from outside
- **Floating elevation**: Raised off the forest floor, enhancing the weightless illusion
- **Total privacy**: The most secluded unit on the Bolt Farm property
- **Arhaus collaboration**: Interior furnished with curated Arhaus pieces
- **Sensory design**: The interplay of reflection, transparency, and forest creates a meditative state

## The Space

The interior is intimate and intentional. A king bed faces the largest glass wall. The kitchenette is minimal but functional — this is a stay about presence, not cooking elaborate meals. Quality linens, curated toiletries, and thoughtful details throughout. The bathroom is compact with premium fixtures.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, positioned among the trees
- **Fire pit**: Dedicated seating area for evening warmth
- **Deck**: Wraps the cabin with views into the canopy
- **Mountain panorama**: Long-range views from the elevated position

## Best For

- Architecture and design enthusiasts seeking a one-of-a-kind stay
- Couples wanting maximum privacy and romance
- Photographers drawn to the mirror-meets-forest visual
- Those who loved Villa Luna and want a similar but distinct experience

## Getting There

Same access as all Bolt Farm properties — SC-11 from Greenville toward Walhalla. Paved roads to the property gate, then a short drive to dedicated parking near your cabin. Flat walk from car to cabin.

A 650 sq ft treehouse with 1,200 sq ft of wrap-around decks in the Blue Ridge Aska Adventure Area, featuring a canopy-level hot tub, swing bed, and panoramic mountain views from every angle.

## The Experience

Canopy Blue sits within the Aska Adventure Area outside Blue Ridge, Georgia — a network of trails, waterfalls, and forest roads that draws hikers and mountain bikers year-round. The treehouse is elevated among mature hardwoods with 1,200 square feet of wrap-around decking that nearly doubles the living space. From the hot tub, the swing bed, or any of the deck's seating areas, you're looking out across layers of Blue Ridge ridgelines.

## What Makes It Special

- **Deck-to-interior ratio**: 1,200 sq ft of outdoor deck vs 650 sq ft interior — the outdoors is the point
- **Swing bed**: Suspended on the deck for afternoon naps with mountain views
- **Canopy-level hot tub**: Positioned at the deck's best vantage point
- **Aska Adventure Area**: Trail network begins minutes from the property
- **Downtown Blue Ridge**: 10-minute drive to restaurants, shops, and the scenic railway

## The Space

The interior features lofted ceilings with exposed beams, a custom dining booth, and beds for six guests across a queen and full bed plus twin bunks in the loft. The full kitchen is well-stocked for cooking. A smart TV with Netflix handles rainy evenings, though the deck and fire pit tend to win.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: On the wrap-around deck with mountain views
- **Swing bed**: Suspended from the deck structure
- **Fire pit**: Ground level with dedicated seating
- **Gas grill**: Full-size Weber on the deck
- **Multiple deck zones**: Morning coffee spot, afternoon reading nook, evening hot tub

## Best For

- Groups or couples who live outdoors and want maximum deck space
- Hikers and trail runners using Aska Adventure Area
- Fall color seekers — the North Georgia mountains peak in late October
- Year-round stays with easy access to Blue Ridge dining

## Getting There

From Atlanta, take GA-400 North to Blue Ridge, then follow signs toward the Aska Adventure Area. The final approach is a paved mountain road with moderate curves. Dedicated parking is steps from the treehouse entrance — no long walks or steep stairs.

A luxury forest-spa cottage with a private cedar soaking tub beneath the trees and a treehouse sauna pavilion, nestled in a grove of mountain laurel and rhododendron just 15 minutes from downtown Asheville.

## The Experience

Asheville Forest Baths is a micro-retreat concept: three luxury cottages and a treehouse sauna pavilion set within 1.5 acres of pristine mountain laurel forest in Fairview, just 15 minutes from downtown Asheville. Every guest receives a complimentary private sauna session in the elevated treehouse sauna — a ritual that sets the tone for the entire stay. The concept blends Japanese forest bathing philosophy with Appalachian forest immersion.

## What Makes It Special

- **Treehouse sauna pavilion**: An elevated sauna structure in the canopy — complimentary private session included
- **Cedar soaking tub**: Private, on your cottage deck beneath the trees
- **Forest bathing philosophy**: The entire property is designed around slowing down and sensory immersion
- **Artisan details**: Hand-painted porcelain sink, custom furnishings, curated toiletries
- **Asheville proximity**: 15 minutes to downtown restaurants and breweries

## The Space

The Cottage is intimate — designed for two. A lofted king bed sits above the main living area, creating a cozy nest with forest views through the windows. The bathroom features the hand-painted porcelain sink and quality fixtures. Custom furnishings throughout give the space a collected, intentional feel rather than a rental aesthetic. The deck with your private cedar soaking tub is the centerpiece.

### Outdoor Living

- **Cedar soaking tub**: Private, on the deck beneath mountain laurel canopy
- **Treehouse sauna**: Elevated pavilion with complimentary private session
- **Deck**: Intimate outdoor living space surrounded by rhododendron
- **Forest paths**: Wander the 1.5-acre mountain laurel grove

## Best For

- Wellness-focused travelers who appreciate ritual and intention
- Couples wanting spa-level experiences in a private setting
- Those who want Asheville access without staying in town
- Year-round stays — the sauna-to-cold-air contrast is best in winter

## Getting There

From Asheville, head east on I-40 toward Fairview. The property is accessed via paved roads in a quiet residential area. Parking is beside your cottage. The entire property is flat and easily walkable.

A treehouse with a full private spa experience — sauna, hot tub, and outdoor shower — nestled in the seclusion of a dense rhododendron forest, just 5 minutes from Highlands' charming main street.

## The Experience

The Bird Nest Treehouse sits in a dense rhododendron forest on the outskirts of Highlands, North Carolina — one of the Southeast's most charming mountain towns at 4,118 feet elevation. The treehouse offers a complete private spa experience: sauna, hot tub, and outdoor shower all within the rhododendron canopy. You're 5 minutes from Highlands' main street with its galleries, restaurants, and shops, but the forest density makes you feel miles from anything.

## What Makes It Special

- **Full spa trifecta**: Sauna + hot tub + outdoor shower in one private setting
- **Rhododendron immersion**: Dense evergreen canopy provides year-round privacy and a tunnel-like forest feel
- **Highlands proximity**: One of the Southeast's premier mountain towns, 5 minutes away
- **Elevation**: At 4,100+ feet, summers are cool and the air is crisp
- **Waterfall density**: Dry Falls, Bridal Veil Falls, and Cullasaja Gorge all within 10 minutes

## The Space

The treehouse is elevated among the rhododendrons with a king bed and quality finishes. The interior is cozy and well-designed — not sprawling, but intentional. Large windows frame the dense green canopy. The bathroom is modern. The real luxury is outside: the sauna, hot tub, and outdoor shower create a spa circuit you can repeat all day.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, nestled in the rhododendron forest
- **Sauna**: Dedicated structure for heat therapy
- **Outdoor shower**: Hot water, surrounded by forest
- **Fire pit**: For evening warmth and atmosphere
- **Forest paths**: Wander the rhododendron tunnels

## Best For

- Spa lovers who want the sauna-hot tub-cold shower ritual in nature
- Couples seeking a romantic Highlands getaway
- Waterfall chasers — the density of falls near Highlands is remarkable
- Spring through fall (rhododendron bloom in June is spectacular)

## Getting There

From Asheville, take US-64 South through Brevard and Cashiers to Highlands. The treehouse is accessed via paved roads in a quiet area just outside town. Parking is near the treehouse with a short walk through the rhododendrons to the entrance.

A two-story treehouse built into a 200-year-old oak canopy at 3,800 feet, with a suspended bridge walkway and a hot tub nestled among the branches.
Alum Cave Trail to Mt. LeConte

## The Experience

Canopy Ridge sits on a private 40-acre parcel bordering Great Smoky Mountains National Park. The treehouse is built into a cluster of mature oaks at the property's highest point — you cross a 30-foot suspended bridge from the parking area to reach the front door. Below you, nothing but forest floor and ferns. Above, the canopy filters light into shifting green patterns all day long.

## What Makes It Special

- **True treehouse construction**: Load-bearing bolts into living trees, not a cabin on stilts
- **Suspended bridge entry**: The 30-foot walkway sways gently and sets the tone immediately
- **Canopy-level hot tub**: Positioned on a reinforced platform among the branches
- **Maximum privacy**: The nearest neighbor is over a mile away through dense forest
- **National Park proximity**: Five major trailheads within 30 minutes

## The Space

The lower level is an open living area with a wood-burning fireplace, leather armchair, and floor-to-ceiling windows on three sides. A spiral staircase leads to the upper sleeping loft with a king bed positioned directly beneath a skylight. The bathroom has a glass-walled rain shower with views into the canopy.

### Amenities Breakdown

- Full kitchen with gas range, dishwasher, and local coffee beans
- Hammock deck suspended between two oaks (separate from the hot tub deck)
- Ground-level fire pit with Adirondack chairs (accessible via a second staircase)
- Bluetooth speaker system throughout
- Starlink internet for remote workers

## Best For

- Nature immersion without sacrificing comfort
- Couples or solo travelers who want genuine seclusion
- Hikers using the Smokies as a base camp
- Spring wildflower season (April–May) and fall color (mid-October)

## Getting There

From Charlotte, take I-40 West through Asheville and continue into the Smokies corridor. The final 3 miles are a paved county road with a gentle grade. The property gate has a keypad — code provided 24 hours before check-in. Cell service is limited on-site but the Starlink connection is reliable.

A 1,175 sq ft geodesic dome on a private mountaintop at Bolt Farm, with breathtaking long-range views, maximum seclusion, and curated romance experiences from a Condé Nast-nominated retreat.

## The Experience

Bolt Farm is an award-winning adults-only retreat perched on a mountaintop in the Upstate South Carolina foothills. Designed by Seth Bolt of NEEDTOBREATHE and built by his father Larry — a licensed custom home builder with 40+ years of experience — every structure on the property reflects both artistic vision and construction expertise. The Luxe Dome is their largest unit: 1,175 square feet of private space including the dome interior, wraparound deck, and fire pit area.

## What Makes It Special

- **Maximum privacy**: Adults-only, each unit completely secluded from others
- **Mountaintop positioning**: Long-range views across the Blue Ridge foothills
- **Curated experiences**: Optional romance packages, candlelit turndowns, in-room bath rituals
- **Condé Nast recognition**: Made the ballot for Traveler's Readers' Choice Awards
- **Architectural intention**: Every detail designed to foster intimacy and reconnection

## The Space

The geodesic dome's geometry creates a dramatic interior volume. A king bed is positioned to face the best view angle. The kitchenette handles light meals and morning coffee. The bathroom is well-appointed with quality fixtures. But the real living happens on the deck — the hot tub, fire pit, and seating areas all face the mountain panorama.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, with mountain views
- **Fire pit**: Dedicated area with comfortable seating
- **Wraparound deck**: Multiple zones for different times of day
- **Mountain views**: Unobstructed long-range vistas

## Best For

- Couples celebrating milestones — anniversaries, honeymoons, proposals
- Design-conscious travelers who appreciate intentional spaces
- Those seeking genuine disconnection with luxury comfort
- Year-round stays — the mountaintop setting is compelling in every season

## Getting There

From Greenville, SC, take SC-11 (Cherokee Foothills Scenic Highway) toward Walhalla. The property is accessed via paved mountain roads. Bolt Farm provides detailed directions and gate access information before arrival. The drive from the gate to your dome is short and paved.

A rustic-modern cabin with a private cedar barrel sauna and year-round hot tub under the stars, set on a wooded acre just a two-minute walk from Shenandoah River Outfitters and 15 minutes from Shenandoah National Park.

## The Experience

Cedar Soak Lodge sits on a wooded acre in the Shenandoah Valley just outside Luray, Virginia — a small mountain town known for its caverns, craft breweries, and proximity to Shenandoah National Park. The cabin blends rustic charm with modern comfort: think shiplap walls, a well-equipped kitchen, and smart TVs alongside a private cedar barrel sauna and year-round hot tub beneath the tree canopy. The property's standout feature is its location — a two-minute walk puts you at Shenandoah River Outfitters, where tubing, kayaking, and fishing launch directly from the riverbank. After a day on the water or hiking the park's ridgeline trails, the sauna-to-hot-tub ritual becomes the evening's main event.

## What Makes It Special

- **Cedar barrel sauna**: Authentic wood-fired sauna reaching 220°F — detox after a day on the trails
- **Year-round hot tub**: Under the stars on the back deck, surrounded by forest
- **Walk to the river**: Shenandoah River Outfitters is a two-minute stroll for tubing, kayaking, and fishing
- **Shenandoah National Park**: Thornton Gap entrance is 15 minutes away with access to Skyline Drive and dozens of trails
- **Local craft scene**: Hawksbill Brewing, wineries, and downtown Luray restaurants within 10–15 minutes

## The Space

The cabin sleeps six across two bedrooms — a king primary and a second room with bunks — plus a full bathroom with modern fixtures. The open-plan living area has a comfortable couch, smart TV, and a kitchen stocked with a Ninja coffee maker, full cookware, and dining for six. The design is rustic-modern: clean lines, warm wood tones, and intentional touches that make it feel curated rather than generic. Life-size Connect Four and cornhole on the lawn add playful energy for groups.

### Outdoor Living

- **Cedar barrel sauna**: Private, wood-heated, seats four comfortably
- **Hot tub**: Year-round, on the back deck beneath the canopy
- **Stone fire pit**: Under string lights for evening gatherings
- **Hammock**: Strung between trees for afternoon reading
- **Weber charcoal grill**: For cookouts on warm evenings
- **Rocking chair porch**: Front deck with morning coffee views

## Best For

- Couples or small groups wanting a sauna-and-soak wellness retreat
- Hikers using the cabin as a Shenandoah National Park basecamp
- River lovers who want tubing and kayaking at their doorstep
- Those seeking a balance of outdoor adventure and evening relaxation

## Getting There

From Richmond, take I-64 West to I-81 South, then US-211 East toward Luray. The cabin is accessed via well-maintained paved roads in a quiet wooded area just outside town. Parking is beside the cabin on a level gravel pad. No steep grades or tight switchbacks — standard sedans handle the route easily year-round.

A 530 sq ft geodesic dome overlooking Fall Branch Falls, bordering the Chattahoochee National Forest and the 300-mile Benton MacKaye Trail — waterfall views from your private hot tub.

## The Experience

This dome resort in Cherry Log, Georgia sits on a property that directly borders the Chattahoochee National Forest and overlooks Fall Branch Falls. The 300-mile Benton MacKaye Trail passes through the property, meaning you can step out of your dome and onto a long-distance hiking trail without driving anywhere. Each dome is 530 square feet, uniquely styled, and positioned for privacy from its neighbors.

## What Makes It Special

- **Waterfall views**: Fall Branch Falls is visible from the property — some domes have direct sightlines
- **Trail access**: The Benton MacKaye Trail is a 5-minute walk from your dome
- **National Forest border**: The Chattahoochee National Forest begins at the property edge
- **Individual styling**: Each dome has a unique interior design theme
- **Hot tub with a view**: Positioned to face the falls or forest depending on the dome

## The Space

At 530 square feet, the dome is cozy but well-designed. A king bed occupies the primary zone with quality linens. The kitchenette handles coffee, snacks, and light meals. Chalets Hemlock — Log Cabin with Sauna
A log cabin chalet with a private indoor sauna and outdoor hot tub on 140 wooded acres, two miles from Old Man's Cave — the epicenter of Hocking Hills hiking.

## The Experience

The Chalets at Old Man's Cave occupy 140 acres of Hocking Hills forest along State Route 664, placing you at the geographic heart of Ohio's most dramatic hiking landscape. Hemlock is a log cabin chalet that sits in a grassy clearing surrounded by woods — private enough to feel secluded, yet just two miles from the Hocking Hills State Park visitors center and the trailhead for Old Man's Cave. The cabin's standout feature is its indoor sauna, a rarity in the region, paired with a private outdoor hot tub steps from the covered porch. After a day exploring sandstone gorges and waterfalls, the sauna-to-hot-tub ritual is the kind of recovery that makes you want to extend your stay.

## What Makes It Special

- **Indoor sauna**: A private sauna inside the cabin — uncommon in Hocking Hills rentals
- **Hot tub on the porch**: Step from the covered porch directly into the hot tub, ideal in winter
- **Two miles from Old Man's Cave**: The closest you can stay to the park's most iconic trail
- **140-acre wooded campus**: Part of the Chalets resort property with forest trails and seasonal pool
- **Log cabin character**: Vaulted ceilings with skylights, wood floors, and a gas fireplace

## The Space

Hemlock is a two-bedroom, two-bathroom log cabin with 1,700 square feet spread across two levels. The main floor features a queen bedroom, full kitchen with stainless appliances, and a living area with vaulted ceilings and skylights that flood the space with natural light. The lower level has a second bedroom with two queen beds and its own full bathroom. The gas fireplace operates seasonally and adds warmth on cool evenings. The kitchen is fully equipped for real cooking — not just reheating — with a full-size range, refrigerator, microwave, and all the utensils you need.

## Best For

- Hikers who want to be steps from the best trails in the Midwest
- Couples or small groups seeking the sauna-and-hot-tub recovery ritual
- Four-season visitors — the cabin works year-round with heat, A/C, and indoor amenities
- Those who want proximity to restaurants and a general store without sacrificing a wooded setting

## Getting There

From Columbus, take US-33 South to Logan, then State Route 664 South. The Chalets campus is directly off SR 664 — a fully paved state highway with no steep grades or unpaved sections. The driveway is flat and paved with parking beside your cabin. The general store is next door, and the state park visitors center is a five-minute drive south on the same road.

A custom-designed luxury A-frame with 20 skylights and soaring windows on a 29-acre private property featuring its own cliffs, caves, and seasonal waterfalls — architect-crafted for groups seeking a premium Hocking Hills experience.

## The Experience

Dunlap Hollow is a luxury retreat property in Rockbridge, Ohio, set on 29 acres of rugged Hocking Hills terrain complete with its own cliffs, recess caves, seasonal waterfalls, and a scenic overlook. The A-Frame is the property's showpiece — a custom-designed structure with soaring windows and 20 skylights that flood the interior with natural light and frame the surrounding forest canopy. Designed by owners Bryant and Amy Gingerich with architectural support from Meredith Higgins, every detail reflects intentional craftsmanship. The suspended wood-burning stove anchors the open living space, while the 6-person hot tub on the deck provides the post-hike recovery ritual. With three dedicated hiking trails starting at your doorstep and Hocking Hills State Park just 15 minutes away, this is a property where the landscape is both the backdrop and the main attraction.

## What Makes It Special

- **20 skylights**: The A-frame geometry combined with skylights creates a cathedral of natural light
- **Private trail system**: Three marked trails on the property — the Overlook Trail to a valley vista, the Crescent Cave Trail through a large recess cave, and the Cliffside Trail along dramatic rock formations
- **Suspended wood-burning stove**: A striking centerpiece that heats the open-plan living space
- **6-person hot tub**: Private deck setting surrounded by forest
- **Architect-designed**: Custom-built in 2021 with warm, elegant finishes throughout
- **29 acres of private wilderness**: Cliffs, caves, and seasonal waterfalls without the state park crowds

## The Space

The A-Frame sleeps 10 across three bedrooms and a third-floor sleeping loft. The main floor features a king bedroom, full-service kitchen with modern appliances, and the dramatic open living area centered on the suspended stove. Two queen bedrooms occupy the second level, while the loft holds a king sunken bed flanked by two single Murphy beds — a space defined by the converging rooflines and those 20 skylights overhead. Two full bathrooms serve the house. Outside, the deck extends the living space with the hot tub, an outdoor propane fireplace with lounging couches, a propane grill, and a dedicated fire pit area. The property is pet-free, maintaining a pristine environment for all guests.

## Best For

- Groups of friends or extended families seeking a premium shared retreat
- Architecture enthusiasts drawn to intentional, custom-designed spaces
- Hikers who want private trails at their doorstep plus proximity to the state park
- Couples celebrating milestones who want the entire property to themselves
- Four-season visitors — the stove and hot tub make winter stays exceptional

## Getting There

From Columbus, take US-33 South toward Logan, then follow State Route 180 toward Rockbridge. Dunlap Road is a paved county road accessible by any vehicle. The property provides gate access information before arrival, and the drive from the road to the A-Frame is short and well-maintained. Rock House and Cantwell Cliffs are 8–10 minutes away, and Old Man's Cave is a 15-minute drive south on paved state highways.

A custom-built luxury shipping container with a built-in waterfall feature, sunk-in hammock, swinging daybed, and hot tub — industrial steel reimagined as a creative mountain retreat in the Blue Ridge foothills.

Green Creek Shipyard 2.0 proves that a 40-foot shipping container can be transformed into something genuinely luxurious. Built by three siblings on family land in the Blue Ridge foothills of North Carolina, the container has been reimagined with a custom waterfall feature, sunk-in hammock, swinging daybed, and a level of finish that belies its industrial origins. It's the closest property to Charlotte on the escape vertical — just 90 minutes door to door.

- **Swinging daybed**: Suspended on the deck for afternoon naps
- **Proximity**: 90 minutes from Charlotte, 30 from Asheville, 4 miles from the Blue Ridge Parkway

## The Space

The container interior is compact but thoughtfully designed. A king bed faces a large glass window — imagine watching sunrise without leaving bed. The glass rain shower is a standout detail in the small bathroom. Storage and surfaces are minimal but sufficient for a weekend stay. The real living space is outside on the expansive deck.

- **Sunk-in hammock**: Recessed into the deck structure
- **Swinging daybed**: Suspended for gentle motion

- Charlotte residents wanting the closest quality escape
- Couples seeking a quick weekend reset
- Year-round stays — the container is fully insulated and climate-controlled

An elevated A-frame treehouse on 48 secluded forest acres with a private wood-fired hot tub, communal sauna, and floor-to-ceiling windows framing the canopy — minutes from Sodus Bay and Finger Lakes wine country.

## The Experience

The Finger Lakes Treehouse Retreat sits elevated among the trees on a 48-acre private forest property in Sodus, New York. These are genuine A-frame structures raised off the ground, blending treehouse adventure with modern luxury. Each unit is designed for couples seeking seclusion without sacrificing comfort — private entrances, no shared walls, and enough distance between cabins that you feel genuinely alone in the woods. The property sits at the northern edge of the Finger Lakes region, minutes from Sodus Bay on Lake Ontario and within easy reach of the Seneca Lake wine trail, Chimney Bluffs State Park, and Bristol Mountain ski resort.

## What Makes It Special

- **Elevated A-frame design**: Raised structures with steep rooflines and floor-to-ceiling glass walls framing the forest canopy
- **Private wood-fired hot tub**: Each unit has its own hot tub — no sharing, no scheduling
- **Communal sauna**: A shared Finnish-style sauna available to all guests on the property
- **48 acres of forest**: Enough space that each treehouse feels completely isolated
- **Year-round destination**: Ski Bristol Mountain in winter, kayak Sodus Bay in summer, tour wineries in fall

## The Space

The A-frame interior maximizes vertical space with soaring ceilings and a lofted sleeping area. A king bed sits in the peak with views through the glass wall into the tree canopy. The main level includes a full kitchen with modern appliances, a spa-like bathroom, comfortable living area, and a laptop-friendly workspace. Climate control keeps things comfortable in every season. The design is clean and modern — warm wood tones, intentional lighting, and quality finishes throughout.

### Outdoor Living

- **Wood-fired hot tub**: Private to your unit, surrounded by forest
- **Fire pit**: Dedicated area with seating for evening fires
- **Outdoor furniture**: Deck seating for morning coffee among the trees
- **Grill**: Outdoor cooking setup for warm-weather meals
- **Forest trails**: Walking paths across the 48-acre property

## Best For

- Couples seeking a romantic escape with genuine privacy
- Wine country visitors wanting a unique home base for Finger Lakes touring
- Winter travelers combining Bristol Mountain skiing with hot tub evenings
- Those who appreciate architectural design in a natural setting

From I-90 (New York State Thruway), take exit 42 toward Geneva/Lyons, then follow Route 14 north toward Sodus. The property is on Pilgrimport Road, accessed entirely via paved roads through flat agricultural countryside. Easy sedan access with parking beside your treehouse. The drive from Rochester is about 45 minutes; from Syracuse, about an hour.

A handcrafted Scandinavian chalet nestled in the Finger Lakes woods with floor-to-ceiling windows, a private sauna, hot tub under the stars, and custom furniture — minutes from Letchworth State Park's gorges and waterfalls.

## The Experience

The Finger Lakes Scandinavian Chalet sits tucked into a wooded hillside near Dansville, New York, offering a design-forward retreat that blends Nordic minimalism with the natural beauty of the western Finger Lakes. This handcrafted home was built with energy efficiency and intentional living at its core — every detail from the custom furniture to the oversized windows connecting you to the surrounding forest has been carefully considered. The property occupies a quiet, secluded setting where light pollution is minimal and the only sounds are wind through the trees and birdsong at dawn. Guests consistently describe the atmosphere as peaceful and restorative, a place where the boundary between indoors and outdoors dissolves through walls of glass framing the forest canopy.

## What Makes It Special

- **Scandinavian design philosophy**: Custom-made furniture, clean lines, and natural materials throughout create a calm, intentional space
- **Private sauna**: A dedicated sauna for post-hike recovery or evening relaxation sessions
- **Hot tub under the stars**: Soak in the hot tub surrounded by forest with zero light pollution for exceptional stargazing
- **Floor-to-ceiling windows**: Oversized glass panels bring the forest inside from every room
- **Pellet stove**: A cozy Scandinavian pellet stove for atmospheric evenings by the fire
- **Heated bathroom floors**: Spa-quality heated floors and towel warmers in all three bathrooms

## The Space

The chalet interior features two bedrooms with queen beds, three full bathrooms with heated floors, and an open-concept living area anchored by the pellet stove. The fully equipped kitchen includes high-end induction appliances, and the living space offers both a smart TV and a projection screen for movie nights. A lower-level game room provides additional entertainment with table games and flexible seating. The design throughout emphasizes warm wood tones, natural light, and Scandinavian simplicity — nothing feels cluttered or overdone.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, set among the trees with unobstructed sky views
- **Sauna**: Dedicated sauna building for heat therapy and relaxation
- **Fire pit**: Stone-ringed with a grill for outdoor cooking and evening gatherings
- **Pond-side trail**: A walking path around the property's pond through mixed hardwood forest
- **Deck**: Spacious deck with seating for morning coffee surrounded by forest

## Best For

- Couples or small groups seeking a design-conscious nature retreat
- Hikers using the property as a base for Letchworth State Park and Finger Lakes Trail
- Wine country visitors exploring nearby Finger Lakes wineries and breweries
- Stargazers and those seeking genuine quiet away from light pollution
- Winter travelers combining sauna sessions with nearby skiing at Swain Resort

## Getting There

From I-390, take exit 4 toward Dansville and follow Route 36 south. The property is accessed via paved roads through rolling farmland and wooded hills. The final approach is a smooth driveway to dedicated parking beside the chalet. Dansville village is a 10-minute drive for groceries and dining. Letchworth State Park — named the best state park in the US in 2025 — is just 15 minutes away. The drive from Rochester is about 50 minutes; from Syracuse, roughly 90 minutes.

The bathroom features heated floors (a luxury you don’t expect in a dome) and modern fixtures. The kitchenette handles coffee and light meals. But the dome is really a sleeping and shelter space — the deck is where you’ll spend your time.

Outdoor Living

  • Hot tub: Private, with mountain views
  • Fire pit: Gas fire table on the deck
  • Gas grill: Full-size for cooking outdoors
  • Deck seating: Multiple chairs and a table oriented toward the view
  • Mountain panorama: Unobstructed long-range views of the Blue Ridge

Best For

  • Couples wanting a unique stay with easy Asheville access
  • First-time glampers who want luxury without roughing it
  • Photographers chasing sunrise and sunset over the Blue Ridge
  • Spring through fall stays (seasonal operation)

Getting There

From Asheville, head west on I-40 toward Clyde. The property is accessed via paved mountain roads with clear signage. Dedicated parking is beside your dome. The Glamping Collective provides detailed directions and check-in instructions via email before arrival.
A boho-styled 20-foot yurt with a private hot tub and hillside views in the heart of Hocking Hills, steps from a shared game barn with pool table and shuffleboard.

## The Experience

The Hummingbird Yurt at Hillside Retreats offers a distinctive glamping experience in the Hocking Hills region of southeastern Ohio. This 20-foot yurt sits on a wooded hillside, providing scenic views from a private deck where you can soak in the hot tub after a day of trail exploration. The property is managed by Hocking Lodging Company, a Superhost operation with consistently high guest ratings. Unlike a traditional cabin, the yurt's circular design and skylight create an immersive connection to the surrounding forest — you fall asleep watching stars through the roof and wake to birdsong filtering through the canvas walls. The electric fireplace adds warmth on cool evenings, and the kitchenette means you can prepare simple meals without leaving your retreat. Hillside Retreats sits in the heart of Hocking Hills, placing you within a short drive of every major trail and waterfall in the region.

## What Makes It Special

- **Private hot tub on the hillside**: Soak under the trees with views of the wooded slope below
- **Boho-styled interior**: A thoughtfully designed circular space with queen bed, skylight, and cozy seating area
- **Game barn access**: A shared pole barn with pool table, shuffleboard, darts, cornhole, and lounge seating
- **Fire pit and grill**: Your own outdoor gathering space with a gas grill for cookouts
- **Year-round comfort**: Climate control, electric fireplace, and full bathroom with glass-enclosed shower

## The Space

The Hummingbird is a single-room yurt with an open floor plan that feels spacious despite its compact footprint. The queen bed anchors the space, with a kitchenette along one wall featuring a two-burner cooktop, microwave, pod coffee maker, and mini refrigerator with freezer. The full bathroom includes a glass-enclosed shower with provided toiletries. Outside, the private deck holds the hot tub, an outdoor loveseat, and a bistro dining set. The shared game barn is a short walk across the property and includes a full bathroom, high-top seating, and a mini fridge — making it a natural gathering spot on rainy afternoons.

## Getting There

From Columbus, take US-33 South toward Logan. The property is located off Stage Road in the Logan area, accessible via fully paved state and county roads. The driveway and parking area are gravel but well-maintained and flat — no steep grades or unpaved mountain roads to navigate. A standard sedan handles the route without issue. The major Hocking Hills trailheads are all within 12 to 20 minutes by car, and the town of Logan offers grocery stores, restaurants, and gas stations within 10 minutes.

A geodesic dome tucked along Webb Creek just off Highway 321, five minutes from the Greenbrier entrance to Great Smoky Mountains National Park — river sounds, a private hot tub, and no mountain roads to navigate.

## The Experience

Greenbrier Creekside Dome delivers a glamping experience that feels immersive without the discomfort. The geodesic structure sits on a wooded, park-like property along Webb Creek, just off Highway 321 — meaning you get the sounds of rushing water and the privacy of a forest setting without navigating any steep mountain roads. The dome is five minutes from the Greenbrier entrance to Great Smoky Mountains National Park, putting some of the park's best trails within a short drive. At night, the hot tub becomes the centerpiece: soak under the stars while creek sounds replace the noise of daily life. The host, a Superhost with over 160 reviews and a 4.85 rating, has dialed in the details — from the full indoor bathroom to the mini-split climate control that keeps the dome comfortable year-round.

## The Space

Inside, the dome is compact but well-equipped for a couples retreat or small group of up to four. A king-size bed anchors the sleeping area, with a full-size sofa sleeper for additional guests. The kitchenette includes a refrigerator, microwave, hot plate, air fryer, and sink — enough to handle breakfast and simple meals without needing to drive into town. A full indoor bathroom with an oversized walk-in shower means you never have to leave the dome for essentials. WiFi keeps you connected, and a smart TV handles evening entertainment when the fire pit and hot tub aren't calling.

Outside, the creekside fire pit is the social hub — gather around it after a day on the trails while Webb Creek provides the soundtrack. The hot tub sits on a private deck, and an outdoor seating area rounds out the space. The property shares a 1.5-acre wooded setting with one other dome, but each unit has its own dedicated area and feels private.

## Best For

- Couples seeking an affordable Smokies glamping experience without mountain road anxiety
- First-time glampers who want modern comforts (indoor bathroom, HVAC, WiFi) in an unconventional structure
- Hikers using Greenbrier as a base camp for the quieter east side of the national park
- Year-round visits — the mini-split HVAC and hot tub make winter stays viable

## Getting There

From Charlotte or Atlanta, take I-40 West to Exit 443 (Highway 321 toward Gatlinburg). The dome is located directly off Highway 321 near Pittman Center — no mountain switchbacks, no gravel roads, no white-knuckle driving. The host provides a keypad code for self check-in. Gatlinburg's restaurants and shops are 10 miles south, and the Greenbrier entrance to the national park is a five-minute drive north.

Two connected treehouses on 11 private acres linked by a swing bridge — the main house and a separate guest pod each with private decks and outdoor showers, just 25 minutes from Nashville.

## The Experience

The Greenbrier Beechhouse occupies 11 private acres of mature hardwood forest in Greenbrier, Tennessee — just 25 minutes north of downtown Nashville. The property consists of two connected treehouses: a main house and a separate guest pod, linked by a swing bridge that sways gently as you cross. Each structure has its own private deck and outdoor shower, creating the feeling of a private compound in the trees.

## What Makes It Special

- **Dual treehouse design**: Main house + guest pod connected by swing bridge
- **11 private acres**: Complete seclusion despite Nashville proximity
- **Outdoor showers**: Both structures have private hot-water outdoor showers
- **Swing bridge connection**: The crossing between structures is an experience in itself
- **Nashville access**: Downtown dining, music, and culture 25 minutes away

## The Space

The main treehouse is a 1-bedroom, 1-bathroom space with a king bed, open-air soaking tub, and quality finishes throughout. The guest pod adds a second bedroom and bathroom with its own private deck and outdoor shower. Together they sleep 4 comfortably with a queen sofa bed in the main house. The design is modern and warm — cedar walls, natural light, and intentional simplicity.

### Outdoor Living

- **Open-air soaking tub**: In the main treehouse, surrounded by forest
- **Outdoor showers**: One on each structure's private deck
- **Swing bridge**: Connecting the two treehouses through the canopy
- **Fire pit**: Ground level with seating
- **EV charger**: Available for electric vehicle owners
- **Private decks**: Each structure has its own outdoor living space

## Best For

- Couples wanting a nature escape with easy Nashville access
- Small groups who appreciate having separate sleeping structures
- Music lovers splitting time between the woods and Broadway
- Year-round stays — the forest setting is beautiful in every season

## Getting There

From Nashville, take I-65 North to the Greenbrier exit. The property is accessed via paved roads with a short drive to dedicated parking. The main treehouse is a flat walk from the car. The swing bridge to the guest pod begins from the main deck.

A private mountain cabin on 13 acres bordering Pisgah National Forest, at the junction of the French Broad River and the Appalachian Trail — hot tub, fireplace, and a swimming hole steps away.

## The Experience

Hot Springs is one of those rare Appalachian Trail towns where the trail literally crosses Main Street. The Treehouse Cabins sit on 13 acres bordering Pisgah National Forest on what the owners call "The Magic Mountain" — just 5 minutes above the historic village. Cabin 4 is the most private of the four units, with a king bed, private hot tub, and views into the national forest. The French Broad River and natural mineral hot springs are minutes away.

## What Makes It Special

- **Appalachian Trail junction**: The AT crosses through Hot Springs — thru-hikers resupply here
- **French Broad River**: Kayaking, tubing, and swimming minutes from the cabin
- **Natural mineral springs**: Hot Springs is named for its geothermal mineral soaking pools
- **Pisgah National Forest border**: 13 acres of private land meeting public wilderness
- **Swimming hole**: On-property creek access for warm-weather dips

## The Space

Cabin 4 is a 4-season structure with a king bed, full kitchen, and modern bathroom. The fireplace provides warmth and atmosphere on cool evenings. The interior is clean and well-maintained — not luxury-tier but comfortable and honest. The real draw is the setting: forest on all sides, mountain views from the porch, and the sound of Spring Creek below.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, on the cabin's deck
- **Fireplace**: Interior, wood-burning
- **Gas grill**: On the porch for cooking outdoors
- **Swimming hole**: Creek access on the property
- **Communal fire pit**: Shared gathering space (optional)

## Best For

- Hikers wanting AT access with comfort at the end of the day
- River lovers — the French Broad is ideal for kayaking and tubing
- Budget-conscious travelers seeking quality under $300/night
- Those who want to soak in natural mineral springs after a hike

## Getting There

From Asheville, take US-25/70 North through Marshall toward Hot Springs. The cabins are 5 minutes above the village on a paved road. The final stretch is a gentle grade with no tight switchbacks. Parking is beside your cabin.

A modern yurt perched high on Ski Mountain in Gatlinburg with panoramic Smoky Mountain views from the deck, a private hot tub, and a wood-burning fireplace under a circular ceiling — three miles from downtown yet worlds away.

## The Experience

Idyllic Yurt sits high on Ski Mountain above Gatlinburg, Tennessee — a modern yurt that blends circular architecture with mountain luxury. The property is just three miles from downtown Gatlinburg's restaurants and shops, yet the elevation and tree cover create a sense of genuine seclusion. From the wraparound deck, the Great Smoky Mountains unfold in layered ridges that shift from green to blue to purple depending on the time of day and the famous mountain haze.

The yurt's circular design creates a warm, enveloping interior that feels fundamentally different from a rectangular cabin. Light enters from every direction, and the wood-burning fireplace at the center anchors the space during cooler months. It sleeps six comfortably across two king bedrooms and a sofa bed, making it ideal for couples or a small group of friends looking for something architecturally distinctive without sacrificing comfort.

## The Space

Two bedrooms each feature king beds and smart TVs, with the primary suite including a jetted soaking tub in the en-suite bathroom. The open living area flows around the central wood-burning fireplace, with a full kitchen equipped for real cooking — not just reheating. Vaulted circular ceilings give the compact footprint an airy, spacious feel that surprises first-time yurt guests.

### Outdoor Living

The deck is where this property truly shines. A private hot tub faces the mountain views, and the gas grill and fire pit extend the living space outdoors. The uncovered deck means unobstructed stargazing on clear nights — Gatlinburg's light pollution is minimal at this elevation. A record player inside and the crackling fire outside set the evening mood.

## Best For

- Couples or small groups seeking a unique architectural stay in the Smokies
- Hikers wanting quick access to Great Smoky Mountains National Park trailheads
- Weekend warriors from Charlotte, Atlanta, or Greenville (all under 3.5 hours)
- Year-round visits — the fireplace and hot tub make winter stays cozy, while summer brings lush canopy and firefly season

## Getting There

From Asheville, take I-40 East to Exit 407 and follow US-441 South into Gatlinburg. Ski Mountain Road climbs steadily but is fully paved with no steep switchbacks. The property is accessed via a paved community road with dedicated parking at the yurt. Standard sedans handle the route comfortably in three seasons; the host recommends all-wheel drive only during active winter storms with ice accumulation.

A luxury treehouse tucked into a ravine in the Blue Ridge Aska Adventure Area, with a California king bed, private hot tub, stone fireplace, and the sound of a creek running below the structure.

## The Experience

Inn The Ravine is the sister treehouse to Canopy Blue, operated by Blue Ridge Treehouse Rentals in the Aska Adventure Area of North Georgia. While Canopy Blue sits high with panoramic views, Inn The Ravine is tucked into a ravine — surrounded by trees on all sides with a creek running below. The effect is intimate and enveloping rather than expansive.

## What Makes It Special

- **Ravine setting**: Nestled low among the trees rather than perched above them
- **Creek soundtrack**: Running water below the structure provides constant ambient sound
- **California king bed**: Larger than standard king — a detail that matters
- **Stone fireplace**: Interior warmth and atmosphere on cool evenings
- **Secluded from sibling**: Despite being on adjacent property to Canopy Blue, fully private

## The Space

The interior is larger than Canopy Blue at approximately 800 sq ft, with a California king in the primary bedroom, a queen in the second, and a twin in the loft. The full kitchen is well-equipped with modern appliances. The stone fireplace anchors the living area. A smart TV with Netflix is available, and WiFi is strong enough for streaming.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, positioned on the deck overlooking the ravine
- **Fire pit**: Ground level near the creek
- **Deck**: Multi-level with seating areas at different elevations
- **Gas grill**: Full-size on the deck
- **Creek access**: Steps lead down to the water from the lower deck

## Best For

- Those who prefer intimate, enclosed forest settings over open views
- Couples or small families wanting a creek-side retreat
- Hikers using the Aska Adventure Area trail network
- Year-round stays — the ravine setting is dramatic in every season

## Getting There

Same access as Canopy Blue — from Atlanta, take GA-400 North to Blue Ridge, then follow signs toward the Aska Adventure Area. The final approach is a paved mountain road. Dedicated parking is steps from the treehouse entrance.

A secluded cabin on a private stretch of the Whitewater River in Nantahala National Forest, with a trail leading to six private waterfalls and a Finnish sauna perched on the riverbank.

## The Experience

Lost Cove sits on a private stretch of the Whitewater River above Cashiers, North Carolina, deep within the Nantahala National Forest. The property's defining feature is a private trail system that leads to six waterfalls — including Bedrock Falls and Exit Falls — accessible only to guests. You can spend an entire day exploring cascades without seeing another person.

## What Makes It Special

- **Six private waterfalls**: A trail system exclusive to the property leads to cascades ranging from intimate slides to dramatic drops
- **Finnish sauna**: A dedicated sauna building perched on the riverbank
- **River frontage**: A private stretch of the Whitewater River with swimming holes
- **Maximum seclusion**: Deep in national forest with no visible neighbors
- **Waterfall density**: More private waterfalls than any other property in the region

## The Space

The cabin is a 3-bedroom, 3.5-bathroom retreat built with quality materials and modern finishes. The primary suite has a king bed with river views. The full kitchen is equipped for extended stays. Living areas are comfortable and well-furnished without being fussy. The real luxury here is the land — the cabin is the base camp for the waterfall trail system.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, with forest and river sounds
- **Finnish sauna**: Standalone building on the riverbank — heat up, then plunge in the river
- **Fire pit**: Stone-ringed near the river
- **Private trail system**: Maintained paths to six waterfalls
- **Swimming holes**: Multiple spots along the private river stretch

## Best For

- Waterfall chasers who want exclusive access
- Sauna enthusiasts who appreciate the heat-then-cold-plunge ritual
- Families or groups who want a multi-day adventure base
- Spring and summer when water flow is highest and swimming is ideal

## Getting There

From Asheville, take US-64 South toward Cashiers. The property is accessed via paved roads with a final stretch of maintained gravel. The drive is scenic and straightforward — no white-knuckle switchbacks. Parking is beside the cabin with a flat walk to the front door.

A secluded adults-only cabin on 200 private acres near Pisgah National Forest, with three on-property waterfalls, optional chef-crafted dinners served beside a cascade, and side-by-side massages in the forest.
Pisgah National Forest — Looking Glass Falls

## The Experience

Meraki Escape occupies 200 private acres in the Blue Ridge Mountains near Brevard, North Carolina — the self-proclaimed "Land of Waterfalls" with over 250 cascades in the surrounding county. The property itself has three private waterfalls, including Forestsong Falls where optional chef-crafted dinners are served on a candlelit table beside the cascade. This is not a cabin with add-ons — it's a curated experience designed around reconnection.

## What Makes It Special

- **Three private waterfalls**: On-property cascades accessible only to guests
- **Waterfall dining**: Optional multi-course chef dinners served beside Forestsong Falls
- **In-cabin massage**: Side-by-side couples massage in your cabin or by the waterfall
- **200 acres of seclusion**: Adults-only, no neighbors, no shared spaces
- **Elevation**: At 2,600 feet, the air is noticeably cleaner and cooler

## The Space

The Ukiyo cabin is designed for intimacy. A king bed faces the forest through large windows. The walk-in shower features rainfall and body jet systems built into natural stone. The fireplace anchors the living area. The full kitchen is stocked with quality cookware and a professional blender. Covered and uncovered porches extend the living space into the forest.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Under the covered porch, surrounded by forest sounds and distant waterfall
- **Fire table**: Gas, on the uncovered porch for stargazing
- **Private trails**: Paths to three on-property waterfalls
- **Forest immersion**: 200 acres of hardwood forest at your doorstep

## Best For

- Couples seeking a curated romantic experience beyond just a cabin
- Honeymoons, anniversaries, and proposals
- Those who want optional luxury services (chef, massage) without leaving the property
- Year-round stays — the elevation keeps summers cool and winters dramatic

## Getting There

From Asheville, head south toward Brevard on US-276. The property is accessed via paved roads with a final stretch of maintained gravel. Meraki provides detailed directions and gate information before arrival. The cabin is a short flat walk from parking.

An eco-friendly treehouse perched above an ancient rhododendron canopy, adjoining 1,000+ acres of conservation land with mountain views and a private hot tub — just 15 minutes from downtown Asheville.

## The Experience

The Cardinal Treehouse is one of six nature-inspired cabins operated by Modcabins, a family-owned Asheville business. What sets it apart is the setting: perched above an ancient rhododendron canopy with 1,000+ acres of conservation land as your backyard. You're 15 minutes from downtown Asheville's restaurants and breweries, but the treehouse feels genuinely remote — surrounded by nothing but protected forest and mountain views.

## What Makes It Special

- **1,000+ acres of conservation buffer**: Protected land surrounds the property — no development coming
- **Rhododendron canopy**: The treehouse sits above a dense, ancient understory that blooms spectacularly in June
- **Eco-friendly build**: Sustainable construction with minimal forest impact
- **Asheville proximity**: Downtown dining, breweries, and River Arts District 15 minutes away
- **Family-owned operation**: Personal attention and local knowledge from the Modcabins team

## The Space

The Cardinal Treehouse is a romantic retreat for two with a king bed, full kitchen, and modern bathroom. The design is warm and nature-inspired — wood tones, quality linens, and large windows framing the canopy. The interior is compact but well-designed, with every detail considered. A loft adds visual interest and additional space.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, with mountain views above the rhododendron canopy
- **Fire pit**: Solo stove style, on the deck or clearing
- **Deck**: Elevated with views into the conservation land
- **Trail access**: Walk directly onto conservation land trails from the property

## Best For

- Couples wanting a treehouse experience with easy Asheville access
- Nature lovers who appreciate protected landscapes and old-growth understory
- Rhododendron bloom season (late May–June) when the canopy explodes with color
- Year-round stays — the conservation land is beautiful in every season

## Getting There

From downtown Asheville, head east approximately 7 miles. The property is accessed via paved roads. Modcabins provides detailed directions and check-in instructions. Parking is near the treehouse with a short walk to the entrance.

A Scandinavian-modern A-frame with soaring angles and big mountain views, adjoining 1,000+ acres of conservation land — with a fenced yard, hot tub, and fire pit, walking distance to downtown Black Mountain.

## The Experience

The Modcabins A-Frame sits in Black Mountain, North Carolina — a quiet mountain town that consistently ranks among the best small towns in the Southeast. The cabin blends vintage camp nostalgia with Scandinavian modern design: soaring A-frame angles, clean lines, and big windows framing mountain views. It's walking distance to downtown Black Mountain's coffee shops, breweries, and restaurants, yet backs up to 1,000+ acres of conservation land.

## What Makes It Special

- **Iconic A-frame geometry**: The steep roofline creates dramatic interior volume and visual impact
- **Scandinavian-modern design**: Clean lines, warm wood, and intentional minimalism
- **Walkable to town**: Downtown Black Mountain is a half-mile stroll
- **Conservation land buffer**: 1,000+ protected acres behind the property
- **Fenced yard**: Rare for a mountain rental — great for dogs or just privacy

## The Space

The A-frame's geometry defines the interior — a lofted king bed in the peak with mountain views through the front glass wall. The main level has a full kitchen, retro electric fireplace, and comfortable living area. The bathroom is modern and well-appointed. The design mixes vintage camp elements (wood paneling, warm lighting) with contemporary comfort (quality linens, smart TV, strong WiFi).

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: In the fenced yard with mountain views
- **Fire pit**: Solo stove style in the yard
- **Fenced yard**: Private outdoor space — unusual for mountain rentals
- **Mountain views**: Visible from the hot tub and through the A-frame's front glass wall
- **Walking trails**: Conservation land access from the backyard

## Best For

- Design lovers who appreciate the A-frame aesthetic
- Couples wanting walkable town access with mountain seclusion
- Dog owners who need a fenced yard
- Those who want Asheville-area quality at a slightly lower price point

## Getting There

From Charlotte, take I-40 West to the Black Mountain exit. The cabin is in a quiet neighborhood within walking distance of downtown. All paved roads, flat terrain, easy parking beside the cabin.

A Scandinavian-designed treehouse elevated among hardwoods on the Cumberland Plateau, with clean Nordic lines, a private hot tub, and views into the forest canopy from every window.

## The Experience

The Scandinavian Treehouse at Terralodge strips away ornamentation and lets the forest do the talking. Elevated among mature hardwoods on the Cumberland Plateau, the structure uses clean lines, natural wood, and oversized windows to create a sense of floating within the canopy. The Nordic design philosophy — simplicity, function, warmth — translates perfectly to a forest setting.

## What Makes It Special

- **Nordic minimalism**: Light wood, clean geometry, and intentional negative space
- **Canopy immersion**: Windows on all sides place you at eye level with the treetops
- **Private hot tub**: Tucked on the deck among the branches
- **Plateau location**: 10-15 degrees cooler than surrounding valleys in summer
- **Proximity to trails**: Fiery Gizzard and Foster Falls within 15 minutes

## The Space

The interior is compact but never cramped. A king bed faces the largest window wall. The kitchen is fully equipped with modern appliances and clean countertops. The living area has a smart TV and comfortable seating, though the deck tends to pull you outside. The bathroom is simple and well-appointed with quality fixtures.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: Private, positioned for forest views
- **Fire pit**: Below the treehouse with dedicated seating
- **Deck**: Wraps the structure with multiple vantage points
- **Forest access**: Trails begin steps from the property

## Best For

- Minimalists who appreciate design restraint
- Couples seeking a quiet plateau retreat
- Trail runners and hikers using South Cumberland as a base
- Fall color season (October–November) when the hardwoods ignite

## Getting There

Same access as all Terralodge properties — I-24 East from Nashville, exit at Monteagle. Paved roads to the property gate, smooth gravel to your parking spot. The treehouse is a short flat walk from the car.

A 1,500 sq ft geodesic dome with a 12-foot panoramic glass wall, Matouk linens, heated floors throughout, and a private hot tub perched on the Cumberland Plateau edge.

## The Experience

Terralodge sits on the Cumberland Plateau near Monteagle, Tennessee, where sandstone cliffs drop into deep gorges carved over millennia. The Luxe Dome is their flagship unit — a 1,500-square-foot geodesic structure that feels more like a modernist glass house than a tent. The 12-foot panoramic window wall frames nothing but forest canopy and plateau sky.

## What Makes It Special

- **Architectural scale**: At 16 feet to the center peak, the dome has the volume of a small cathedral
- **Panoramic glass wall**: Triangular double-paned panels create a geometric frame for the forest
- **Heated floors throughout**: Radiant warmth from bathroom to living area — no cold feet at 2 AM
- **Double-sided fireplace**: Visible from both the living area and bedroom zone
- **Matouk linens**: Hotel-grade bedding you'll notice immediately

## The Space

The open floor plan flows from a full kitchen with island seating into a living area centered on the fireplace. The sleeping zone occupies the far curve of the dome with a king bed positioned to face the glass wall. The bathroom features heated floors, a rain shower, and Terralodge-branded robes. A 65-inch TV with Sonos surround is available but feels beside the point.

### Outdoor Living

- **Private hot tub**: On the deck, surrounded by forest
- **Fire pit**: Stone-ringed with Adirondack seating
- **Outdoor shower**: Hot water, open to the canopy
- **Deck**: Wraps the dome with multiple seating areas

## Best For

- Design-forward travelers who want space and light
- Couples or small groups seeking plateau solitude
- Hikers tackling Fiery Gizzard or Savage Gulf
- Year-round stays — heated floors make winter compelling

## Getting There

From Nashville, take I-24 East toward Chattanooga and exit at Monteagle. The property is accessed via paved roads with clear signage. The final stretch is a smooth gravel drive to dedicated parking beside your dome. No steep grades or tight switchbacks.

A modern timber yurt with a cathedral glass dome skylight, hidden near Skyline Drive in the Shenandoah Mountains, with a private hot tub on an elevated deck overlooking the valley.

## The Experience

Skyline Yurt sits in the mountains near Front Royal, Virginia — just an hour from Washington, DC but feeling like another world entirely. The structure is a modern interpretation of the traditional yurt: timber-framed, insulated for four seasons, and crowned with a massive glass dome skylight that fills the interior with natural light and frames the night sky.

## What Makes It Special

- **Glass dome skylight**: The circular ceiling window is the centerpiece — stars at night, dappled light by day
- **Round architecture**: The yurt's geometry creates a warm, enveloping space unlike any rectangular cabin
- **Elevated hot tub deck**: Private, with mountain views stretching across the valley
- **Activity density**: Archery, pool table, PS5, fire pit — rainy days are covered
- **Shenandoah proximity**: Skyline Drive entrance is 15 minutes away

## The Space

The yurt sleeps 8 across three bedrooms and two bathrooms, but works beautifully for couples who want space to spread out. The main living area has vaulted ceilings rising to the glass dome, a wood-burning stove, and a full gourmet kitchen with island seating. A loft above holds the pool table. The 75-inch smart TV has streaming services, but the stove and skylight tend to win the evening.

### Outdoor Living

- **Hot tub**: 7-person, on an elevated deck with mountain views
- **Fire pit**: Stone-ringed with premium seating
- **Archery range**: Full setup with equipment provided
- **Cornhole and lawn games**: On the flat clearing beside the yurt

## Best For

- Couples or small groups wanting a unique architectural stay
- DC/Richmond residents seeking a quick mountain escape
- Old Rag hikers who want luxury after the scramble
- Four-season stays — the wood stove makes winter magical

## Getting There

From Richmond, take I-66 West or US-211 toward Front Royal. The yurt is accessed via paved county roads with a short gravel drive to dedicated parking. No steep grades. The property provides detailed directions and a gate code 24 hours before check-in.

Anchored 20 feet in the air on massive timber posts, accessed by a 38-foot canopy bridge from the hillside, with a private hot tub on the front deck overlooking dense Appalachian forest.

## The Experience

The Tuscany Treehouse sits on a wooded hillside near Hico, West Virginia, within striking distance of New River Gorge National Park. The structure is anchored 20 feet above the forest floor on heavy 12x12 timber posts, and you reach it by crossing a 38-foot canopy bridge from the adjacent hillside. The bridge sways slightly underfoot — a gentle reminder that you've left the ground behind.

## What Makes It Special

- **38-foot canopy bridge**: The entry experience sets the tone for the entire stay
- **Elevated perspective**: At 20 feet up, you're in the mid-canopy with birds and squirrels
- **Dual decks**: Front and back decks offer different forest views and light exposures
- **Hot tub placement**: On the front deck, surrounded by nothing but trees
- **National Park proximity**: New River Gorge Bridge and major trailheads within 20 minutes

## The Space

The interior has a Tuscan-inspired warmth with cedar-wood walls and quality furnishings. A king bed occupies the main sleeping area with views through large windows into the canopy. The full kitchen is compact but well-equipped for cooking real meals. The bathroom is modern and clean with a standard shower.

### Outdoor Living

- **Front deck**: Hot tub, seating area, and the primary view corridor
- **Back deck**: Quieter, morning sun, good for coffee
- **Fire pit**: Ground level, accessible via stairs from the treehouse
- **Canopy bridge**: 38 feet of suspended walkway connecting to the hillside parking area

## Best For

- Adventure travelers using New River Gorge as a base camp
- Climbers and rafters who want comfort after a day on the rock or river
- Couples seeking a unique entry experience (that bridge)
- Spring through fall — the canopy is most dramatic with leaves

## Getting There

From Charleston, WV, take US-19 South toward Fayetteville/Hico. The property is accessed via paved roads with a short gravel drive to the hillside parking area. The canopy bridge begins at the parking level — no stairs to reach it. Cell service is adequate in the area.
